rulings.Case Title: JOY ANDERSON VS. CITIAPARTMENTS, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement And Certification Of A Settlement Class
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, line 1. PLAINTIFFs JOY ANDERSON and NICOLAS HARR's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement And Certification Of A Settlement Class Hearing Required. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BRYAN et al VS. JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. AS SUCCESSOR IN et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, line 2. DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer to the 1st and 3rd Causes of Action is OVERRULED. Defendants' demurrer to the 2nd and 4th Causes of Action is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Defendants' demurrer to the 5th Cause of Action is sustained without leave to amend re statute of limitations. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DIANE SCHOBER et al VS. KAMRAN ARDEBILCHI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of The Courts January 16, 2015, Order Granting Defendants Demurrer To Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint Without Leave To Amend As To Plaintiffs Second Cause Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, line 3. PLAINTIFFs JOAN ESPOSITO, URAIWAN TORSAP, FAHD BINHAMAD, KHALID ALHABESH, UTHUMPORN DRAPER, DANIELLE MCGEE and LOUIS GAMACHE's Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court's January 16, 2015, Order Granting Defendants Demurrer To Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint Without Leave To Amend As To Plaintiffs Second Cause Of Action DENIED. Plaintiffs failed to show that failure to exhaust administrative remedies can be excused because Plaintiffs present no new facts to indicate that it is clear that exhaustion would be futile. The Court does not consider Plaintiffs' argument related to CCP § 473(b) because it was raised for the first time in Reply. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PHILLIP COLLINS et al VS. HAYLEE YEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiff Genesio Lobatoâ¿¿S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two, Plaintiff Thomas Mcvittyâ¿¿S Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And Plaintiffs Phillip Collinsâ¿¿, Genesio Lobatoâ¿¿S, Thomas Mcvittyâ¿¿S, And Gary Cheatleâ¿¿S Production Of Documents In Response To
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, line 4. DEFENDANTs HAYLEE YEE and SUZANNE YEE's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Genesio Lobatoâ Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two, Plaintiff Thomas Mcvittyâ Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And Plaintiffs Phillip Collinsâ, Genesio Lobatoâ, Thomas Mcvittyâ, And Gary Cheatleâ's Production Of Documents Defendants' Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Production of Documents and Special Interrogatories and for Sanctions is GRANTED pursuant to Judge Pro Tem's Report and Recommendation. Defendants' motion to compel further responses to their Requests for Production of Documents, Set 1, No. 12 is granted. Defendants' motion to compel further responses from Plaintiff Lobato to their Special Interrogatories, Set 2, Nos. 120-122, 125-128, 133, 139, 142-145, 148-150 is moot because Plaintiff served responses to those interrogatories when filing the opposition to Defendants' motion. Defendants' motion to compel further responses from Plaintiff Lobato to their Special Interrogatories, Set 2, Nos. 151-155 is GRANTED NOs. 156-158 have been withdrawn. Defendants' motion to compel further responses form Plaintiff McVitty to their Requests for Production of Documents, Set 2, No. 46 is GRANTED. Request No. 47 was withdrawn. Sanction of $1,315.50 is GRANTED against Plaintiffs and their counsel jointly and severally, payable to Defendants no later than May 6, 2015. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROJER ROMERO VS. HAZEL BRUNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have Plaintiffs Request For Admissions To Defendants Hazel And Zahra Bruner Deemed Admitted And Request For Sanctions Under Civ. Code Section C.C.P. § 2033.280
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, line 5. PLAINTIFF ROJER ROMERO's Motion To Have Plaintiff's Request For Admissions To Defendants Hazel And Zahra Bruner Deemed Admitted And Request For Sanctions GRANTED. No Opposition filed. Request for Admissions, Set One is deemed admitted. $1,865 in sanctions granted. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ELITISI SEKONA VS. WELLS FARGO HOME MORTGAGE, INC et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, line 6. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the 1st and 2nd Causes of Action. Homeowners' Bill of Rights cannot apply retroactively. Defendant's Demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to the 3rd and 4th Causes of Action. Plaintiff to plead with specificity for UCL claim. Plaintiff to plead more facts to show legal duty owed by Defendant to Plaintiff. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 2541 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC VS. CHERK YEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, line 7. DEFENDANT CHERK YEE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for declaratory relief because no actual controversy exists and there is no need of determination of future rights. (See Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604; Babb v. Superior Court (1971) 3 Cal.3d 841, 848.). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 2541 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC VS. CHERK YEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, line 7. DEFENDANT PUI YEE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED IN PART WITH LEAVE TO AMEND and OVERRULED IN PART. The demurrer is sustained as to 1st Cause of Action. Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for declaratory relief because there is no ripe issue and there is no need of determination of future rights. (See Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604; Babb v. Superior Court (1971) 3 Cal.3d 841, 848.)The Demurrer is overruled as to 2nd Cause of Action. The facts pleaded by P are sufficient to state cause of action for ejectment. (Baugh v. Consumers Associates, Limited (1966) 241 Cal.App.2d 672, 675.). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTELOPE HALAWAN PROPERTIES, LLC VS. LEYDI GARCIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiff To Compel Defendant To Respond To Written Discovery And Produce Documents; Deeming Matters Admitted; And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, line 8. PLAINTIFF ANTELOPE HALAWAN PROPERTIES, LLC's Motion Compel Defendant To Respond To Written Discovery And Produce Documents; Deeming Matters Admitted; And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions GRANTED. No Opposition filed. Defendant shall provide responses to Form Interrogatories - UD, Form Interrogatories - General, Special Interrogatories, and Requests for Production of Documents within 5 days of notion of entry or order. Defendant shall also produce responsive documents. Request for Admission, Set One, is deemed admitted. Sanctions are granted against Defendant and Defendant's Counsel for $1,360. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LINCOLN BANCORP LLC VS. ZACARIAS TAPIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, line 10. DEFENDANT ZACARIAS TAPIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. There is a disputed issue of material fact as to whether a notice of entry is required. See response to Special Interrogatories, first set, at pg. 3, line 21. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ELLIS LARKIN, LLC VS. CHONG T TRAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleading
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, line 12. DEFENDANT SON NGUYEN's Motion For Judgment On The Pleading DENIED. There are material factual issues that require evidentiary resolution as to whether there was a sublease or assignment involved. (Schabarum v. California Legislature (1998) 60 Cal.App.4th 1205, 1216.). = (501/REQ)
